title: Context Entry Points
one_liner: Every relevant subsystem has a README. Without one, the agent has to reverse-engineer purpose from code.
problem: Agents (and new humans) drop into the repo at an arbitrary file. Without a per-module README they have to infer purpose, ownership, intended entry points and the gotchas from filenames and grep — burning tokens and frequently guessing wrong about which file is canonical, which is legacy, and which is a trap.
solution: Add a short README at every meaningful module/feature boundary: purpose in one paragraph, the entry points, the typical workflows (call X then Y), known pitfalls, and pointers to related modules and ADRs. Keep it boring and current — three honest paragraphs beat thirty stale ones.
